basic_details.job_title: CSIR CFTRI Project Associate Recruitment 2026 - Apply Online
basic_details.organization_name: Central Food Technological Research Institute (CSIR CFTRI)
basic_details.short_information: Central Food Technological Research Institute (CSIR CFTRI) has released an official notification for the recruitment of 1 Project Associate – I Posts. Interested and eligible candidates can apply Online through the official CSIR CFTRI website. The last date to submit the application form is 15-07-2026. In this article, you'll find the Project Associate – I recruitment details, including eligibility criteria, age limit, salary structure, selection process, application steps, and direct links to the official notification and application form.

application_fee.all_fee: No application fee is mentioned in the official notification.

age_limit.details: Maximum Age: 50 years Age Relaxation: SC/ST/PwBD/Women: 05 years OBC: 03 years

qualification.education_qualification: Bachelor's degree in Mechanical/Chemical Engineering or Technology
qualification.details: Project Associate - I: Bachelor's degree in Mechanical/Chemical Engineering or Technology. Desirable: Experience in studying granular flows, using Discrete Element Methods and open source simulation tools like LAMMPS and LIGGGHTS, and post-processing tools.

salary_details.pay_scale: Rs. 37,000/- plus HRA or Rs. 30,000/- plus HRA
salary_details.details: Rs. 37,000/- plus HRA to scholars who are selected through (a) National Eligibility Tests – CSIR-UGC NET including Lectureship (Assistant Professorship) or GATE or (b) A selection process through National level examinations conducted by Central Government Departments And their Agencies and Institutions. Rs. 30,000/- plus HRA for those who do not fall under the above criteria.

how_to_apply: Applications must be submitted online on or before 15-07-2026.
To apply directly, use the Apply Online link provided in the Important Links section.
Alternatively, candidates may visit the official website of CSIR CFTRI to complete the application.
Fill out the application form carefully with correct personal, educational, and contact details.
Upload required documents such as photograph, signature, and certificates in the prescribed format.
Pay the application fee online, if applicable.
Review all details carefully before submitting the form.
Take a printout or save a copy of the submitted application for future reference.

content_sections.heading: CSIR CFTRI Project Associate – I Vacancy 2026 Notification PDF
content_sections.text: Candidates who are interested in applying for the CSIR CFTRI Recruitment 2026 should first download and carefully read the official notification PDF. The notification contains complete details about vacancy distribution, eligibility criteria, age limit, stipend/salary, selection process, important dates, and application instructions. Reading the official document is important to ensure that you meet all requirements before submitting the application form. Download CSIR CFTRI Project Associate – I Job 2026 Notification PDF

content_sections.heading: CSIR CFTRI Project Associate – I Important Dates 2026
CSIR CFTRI Project Associate – I Selection Process 2026
How to Apply
content_sections.text: Applications must be submitted online on or before 15-07-2026. To apply directly, use the Apply Online link provided in the Important Links section. Alternatively, candidates may visit the official website of CSIR CFTRI to complete the application. Fill out the application form carefully with correct personal, educational, and contact details. Upload required documents such as photograph, signature, and certificates in the prescribed format. Pay the application fee online, if applicable. Review all details carefully before submitting the form. Take a printout or save a copy of the submitted application for future reference.

content_sections.heading: CSIR CFTRI Project Associate – I Recruitment 2026 - Important Links

faq.question: Q1. What is the last date to apply for the CSIR CFTRI Project Associate – I recruitment?
faq.answer: The last date to submit the online application form for the Project Associate – I position at CSIR CFTRI is 15th July 2026.

faq.question: Q2. What is the educational qualification required for the Project Associate – I post?
faq.answer: Candidates must possess a Bachelor's degree in Mechanical or Chemical Engineering or Technology to be eligible for the Project Associate – I position.

faq.question: Q3. What is the age limit for the Project Associate – I role?
faq.answer: The maximum age limit for the Project Associate – I position is 50 years, with relaxations applicable for reserved categories.

faq.question: Q4. What is the salary offered for the Project Associate – I position?
faq.answer: The salary offered is Rs. 37,000/- plus HRA or Rs. 30,000/- plus HRA, depending on the selection criteria and candidate's eligibility through national-level tests.

faq.question: Q5. How will candidates be selected for the Project Associate – I role?
faq.answer: The selection process involves shortlisting by a designated committee followed by an interview.

faq.question: Q6. Is there any experience required for this position?
faq.answer: While a Bachelor's degree is the minimum qualification, experience in studying granular flows, using Discrete Element Methods, and open-source simulation tools is considered desirable.

faq.question: Q7. What is the application mode for this recruitment?
faq.answer: Candidates are required to apply online through the official CSIR CFTRI website.

faq.question: Q8. What is the total number of vacancies available?
faq.answer: There is one vacancy for the Project Associate – I post.

faq.question: Q9. Are there any age relaxations for specific categories?
faq.answer: Yes, age relaxations are provided: 5 years for SC/ST/PwBD/Women and 3 years for OBC candidates.

faq.question: Q10. Where can I find the official notification and apply online?
faq.answer: You can find the links to the official notification and the online application form in the "Important Links" section of this article.
